name: Tomato
description: One of the most popular vegetables in Mauritius. Thrives in warm weather and produces abundantly from winter to summer.

name: Brinjal (Eggplant)
description: A warm-season crop that thrives in the Mauritius summer heat. Popular in local cuisine.

name: Chili Pepper
description: A Mauritius staple in every household garden. Grows well year-round in warm conditions.

name: Cucumber
description: Fast-growing vine that loves the Mauritius summer heat. Requires a trellis or support.

name: Bitter Gourd
description: Extremely popular in Mauritian cuisine. Thrives in tropical heat on a trellis or fence.

name: Ridge Gourd
description: Popular local vegetable grown on trellises. Also used as loofah when dried.

name: Pumpkin
description: Space-demanding but highly productive. Grows well in most Mauritius regions.

name: Cabbage
description: A cool-season crop best grown during Mauritius winter in the central plateau or south.

name: Lettuce
description: Fast-growing cool-season green. Best grown in Mauritius winter, especially in higher regions.

name: Spinach
description: Nutritious cool-season green. Grows quickly in Mauritius winter months.

name: French Beans
description: Quick-growing legume that fixes nitrogen in the soil. Very popular in Mauritius.

name: Long Beans (Gros Pois)
description: Heat-tolerant bean that thrives in Mauritius summer. Needs trellis. Very productive.

name: Okra (Lalo)
description: A Mauritius staple. Extremely heat-tolerant and produces abundantly in summer.

name: Sweet Potato (Patate Douce)
description: Easy-to-grow root vegetable that spreads as ground cover. Very low maintenance.

name: Spring Onion
description: Fast-growing essential herb/vegetable in Mauritius cooking. Grows in both seasons.

name: Radish
description: One of the fastest vegetables to grow. Ready in just 3-4 weeks. Great for beginners.

name: Corn (Maize)
description: Tall, space-efficient when grown in rows. Part of the Three Sisters companion planting group.

name: Basil
description: Essential herb in Mauritian and Mediterranean cooking. Repels pests when grown near tomatoes.

name: Coriander (Dhania)
description: Cornerstone of Mauritian cuisine. Best grown in cooler months in shaded spots.

name: Mint
description: Vigorous perennial herb. Grow in containers to control spreading. Used widely in Mauritian cooking.

name: Thyme
description: Drought-tolerant perennial herb. Excellent companion plant and insect repellent.

name: Rosemary
description: Mediterranean herb that adapts well to Mauritius dry/warm coastal areas.

name: Parsley
description: Slow to germinate but very productive biennial. Best in Mauritius winter.

name: Lemongrass
description: Tropical grass herb used widely in Mauritian teas and cooking. Repels mosquitoes.

name: Chives
description: Mild onion-flavored herb. Easy to grow in pots or borders. Deters aphids.

name: Papaya
description: Very fast-growing tropical fruit tree. Can produce fruit within 7-9 months from seed.

name: Pineapple
description: Mauritius pineapples are world-famous. Plant crowns or slips. Takes 18 months to first harvest.

name: Passion Fruit
description: Vigorous climbing vine producing delicious fruit. Needs a strong trellis or fence.

name: Watermelon
description: Space-hungry but rewarding. Best grown in the warm north and west of Mauritius.

name: Strawberry
description: Grows well in Mauritius winter in the cooler central and southern regions.

name: Marigold
description: The ultimate companion plant. Repels nematodes, aphids and whitefly. Grows anywhere.

name: Sunflower
description: Fast-growing annual that attracts pollinators. Can be grown as trellis for climbing plants.

name: Zinnia
description: Colorful, heat-loving annual. Excellent pollinator attractant. Easy for beginners.

name: Bougainvillea
description: Iconic Mauritius ornamental climber. Almost no care needed once established.

name: Hibiscus
description: National flower of Mauritius. Tropical perennial flowering shrub. Attracts butterflies.

name: Taro (Dasheen)
description: Traditional Mauritian staple (songe). Grown in wet, low-lying areas. Both leaves and tubers used.

name: Carrot
description: Root vegetable best grown in Mauritius winter in loose, deep soil.

name: Onion
description: Essential kitchen vegetable grown in Mauritius winter. Long growing season.

name: Garlic
description: Plant individual cloves in winter. Ready to harvest when tops dry. Great pest deterrent.
